snp1.3:1.1: Renouncing violence for all living beings,
snp1.3:1.2: harming not even one of them,
snp1.3:1.3: you would not wish for offspring, so how a companion?
snp1.3:2.1: For a person by nature entangled there are affections;
snp1.3:2.2: on the heels of affection, this pain.
snp1.3:2.3: Seeing the drawback born of affection,
snp1.3:3.1: One whose mind is enmeshed in sympathy for friends & companions,
snp1.3:5.1: As a deer in the wilds, unfettered,
snp1.3:5.2: goes for forage wherever it wants:
snp1.3:6.1: In the midst of companions —when staying at home, when going out wandering—
snp1.3:6.3: Valuing the freedom that no one else covets,
snp1.3:7.1: There is sporting & love in the midst of companions,
snp1.3:7.3: Feeling disgust at the prospect of parting from those who’d be dear,
snp1.3:8.1: Without resistance in all four directions,
snp1.3:9.1: They are hard to please, some of those gone forth,
snp1.3:9.2: as well as those living the household life.
snp1.3:9.3: Being unconcerned with the offspring of others,
snp1.3:10.2: like a kovilara tree that has shed its leaves,
snp1.3:10.3: the enlightened one, cutting all household ties,
snp1.3:11.1: If you gain an astute companion,
snp1.3:12.1: If you don’t gain an astute companion,
snp1.3:12.3: wander alone like a king renouncing his kingdom,
snp1.3:12.4: like the elephant in the Mataṅga wilds, [his herd].
snp1.3:13.2: Those on a par, or better, should be chosen as friends.
snp1.3:13.3: If they’re not to be found, living faultlessly,
snp1.3:14.3: clinking, clashing, two on an arm,
snp1.3:15.1: [Thinking:] “In the same way, if I were to live with another,
snp1.3:15.2: there would be conversation or attachment.”
snp1.3:16.1: Because sensual pleasures, elegant, honeyed, & charming,
snp1.3:16.2: bewitch the mind with their manifold forms—
snp1.3:16.3: seeing this drawback in sensual strings—
snp1.3:17.2: disease, an arrow, a danger for me.”
snp1.3:17.3: Seeing this danger in sensual strings,
snp1.3:19.1: As a great white elephant, with massive shoulders,
snp1.3:19.3: lives in the wilds wherever he wants,
snp1.3:20.1: “There’s no way that one delighting in company
snp1.3:20.3: Heeding the words of the Kinsman of the Sun,
snp1.3:21.2: the sure way attained, the path gained,
snp1.3:21.3: [realizing:] “Unled by others, I have knowledge arisen,”
snp1.3:22.1: With no greed, no deceit, no thirst, no hypocrisy—
snp1.3:22.3: with no inclinations for all the world, every world,
snp1.3:23.2: disregarding the goal, intent on the discordant way.
snp1.3:23.3: Don’t associate yourself with someone heedless & hankering.
snp1.3:24.1: Consort with one who is learned, who maintains the Dhamma,
snp1.3:24.3: Knowing the meanings, subdue your perplexity,
snp1.3:25.1: Free from longing, finding no pleasure in the world’s sport, ardor, or sensual bliss,
snp1.3:27.1:
“This is a bondage.
There’s little happiness here,
snp1.3:27.2: next to no enjoyment, all the more suffering & pain.
snp1.3:27.3:
This is a boil”:
Knowing this, circumspect,
snp1.3:28.2: like a fish in the water tearing a net,
snp1.3:28.3: like a fire not coming back to what’s burnt,
snp1.3:30.2: like a coral tree that has shed its leaves,
snp1.3:30.3: going forth in the ochre robe,
snp1.3:31.1: Showing no greed for flavors, not wanton,
snp1.3:31.2: going from house to house for alms
snp1.3:31.3: with mind unenmeshed in this family or that,
snp1.3:33.1: Turning your back on pleasure & pain,
snp1.3:34.1: With persistence aroused for the highest goal’s attainment,
snp1.3:34.2: with mind unsmeared, not lazy in action,
snp1.3:34.3: firm in effort, with steadfastness & strength arisen,
snp1.3:35.2: constantly living the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma,
snp1.3:35.3: comprehending the danger in states of becoming,
snp1.3:36.1: Intent on the ending of craving & heedful,
snp1.3:36.2: neither drooling nor dumb, but learned, mindful,
snp1.3:37.1: Unstartled, like a lion at sounds.
snp1.3:37.2: Unsnared, like the wind in a net.
snp1.3:37.3: Unsmeared, like a lotus in water:
snp1.3:38.1: Like a lion—forceful, strong in fang,
snp1.3:38.2: living as a conqueror, the king of beasts—
snp1.3:39.1: At the right time consorting with the release through goodwill, compassion, empathetic joy, equanimity,
snp1.3:39.3: unobstructed by all the world, any world,
snp1.3:40.1: Having let go of passion, aversion, delusion;
snp1.3:40.3: unfazed at the ending of life,
snp1.3:41.1: People follow & associate for a motive.
snp1.3:41.2: Friends without a motive these days are rare.
snp1.3:41.3: They’re shrewd for their own ends, & impure.
